vocabulary: Chat-core is highly token-fluent: framer-motion props are built from MOTION.*/EASE_*/SPRING_* (popovers enter with y:4/scale:0.98 over DURATION_POPOVER + EASE_DECELERATE; TurnGroup work rows stagger 0.045s with SPRING_ROW_ENTRY; icon swaps go through the shared BlurSwap — sidebar toggle, Auto/Approve shield, queue cancel, goal play/pause), and Tailwind utilities use the duration-*/ease-* token scale. Entrances are CSS keyframes animate-fade-in (200ms, opacity + 2px rise) and animate-roll-in (160ms, 6px rise) — opacity+translate only, no blur, so the area's entrance language is drier than the stated target vocabulary, and 160ms sits off the duration token scale. The dominant expand/collapse idiom is animated gridTemplateRows 0fr↔1fr with EASE_EMPHASIZED at MOTION.panel/route — it appears at 10+ sites app-wide, so it is a shared-idiom decision, not a one-component bug. Ambient status (shineText 2.2s sweep, animate-spin, the data-thinking rim variants, BudgetDial arc transitions on CSS vars) is CSS-driven and continuous; hover popovers in the composer toolbar are hand-rolled per component with diverging hide delays (80/120/200ms) and only some of them get entrance motion.

findings.current: Work-block header reveal and body collapse both animate gridTemplateRows: "0fr" ↔ "1fr" (lines 145-147 header at MOTION.panel; lines 173-179 body at MOTION.route = 360ms). The body collapse auto-fires on every run completion (auto-collapse effect, line 73-76) over the largest subtree in the app — the full interim tool/reasoning list — forcing layout recalc of the message column every frame.
findings.proposal: This is the app-wide accordion idiom (10+ sites), so fix it as a shared primitive: measure the content once at toggle time and animate clip-path inset + opacity/blur on the content while FLIP-translating the elements below (motion `layout` on the segment container), or at minimum profile it on a long transcript per the prior 'GPU props only' lesson. Independently, drop the body collapse from MOTION.route (360ms — reserved for pane slides; in-content collapse should not exceed ~300ms) to MOTION.panel (220ms) to match the header reveal.

findings.current: ReasoningMessage expand: motion.div with initial={{ gridTemplateRows: "0fr", opacity: 0 }} → animate 1fr, transition { duration: MOTION.panel, ease: EASE_EMPHASIZED }. Animating grid-template-rows triggers layout every frame, and the reasoning body can be long markdown.
findings.proposal: Same shared-idiom fix as TurnGroup — clip-path/measured-height reveal or a verified-jank-free shared Collapse primitive. Lower frequency than TurnGroup (user-initiated disclosure), so medium.

findings.current: Sidebar/right-panel toggle animates layout properties: <main> uses transition-[left,right] duration-route ease-emphasized (line 126) and the header animates transition-[padding-left] between pl-[18px] and pl-[128px] (lines 53-54). The whole chat column (messages, composer) reflows every frame for 360ms.
findings.proposal: Pane-resize layout animation is sometimes unavoidable, but at minimum the header padding-left tween is replaceable with a transform: translateX on the title row (GPU). For the main pane, consider animating the sidebar itself with transform + width snap (or verify frame timing on a long transcript before accepting the layout cost — this is the exact class of jank flagged in the project's motion lessons).

findings.current: Jump-to-bottom control: <AnimatePresence mode="wait"> with key={unreadCount > 0 ? "unread" : "chevron"} — when the chevron swaps to the '{n} new' pill (a co-located swap in the same slot), the old button fully exits (140ms) before the new one enters, leaving an empty frame mid-transition. mode="wait" is only correct here for the show/hide case.
findings.proposal: Split concerns: keep AnimatePresence (no mode="wait") for visibility with the current opacity/y/scale enter-exit, and swap the inner content (chevron vs count) with BlurSwap keyed on unreadCount > 0 inside a single persistent button, letting the shell's color/width change via CSS transition — same pattern as the send/stop button.

findings.current: Queue row exit animates layout properties: exit={{ opacity: 0, height: 0, marginTop: 0, marginBottom: 0 }} (line 47) so neighbors slide up — height/margin animation triggers layout each frame.
findings.proposal: Use <AnimatePresence mode="popLayout"> (the established fix per the project's 'popLayout lists' lesson): exiting row pops out of flow with the target list-row exit (opacity + scale 0.96 + blur(4px)), and the sibling rows' existing `layout` props FLIP them up with transforms. Card-level `layout` already smooths the container.

findings.current: Loop hover popover is a plain conditional portal — `{open && coords && createPortal(<div …surface-popover…>)}` — it pops in/out with zero transition, while the two sibling toolbar popovers (BudgetDial, ModelReasoningPicker) enter with opacity/y:4/scale:0.98 over DURATION_POPOVER + EASE_DECELERATE.
findings.proposal: Wrap in AnimatePresence + motion.div with the exact BudgetDial recipe (initial {opacity:0, y:4, scale:0.98}, transition { duration: DURATION_POPOVER, ease: EASE_DECELERATE }, transformOrigin "bottom left").

findings.current: Goal hover popover is also a bare conditional portal with no entrance/exit — same toolbar, same surface-popover class, but instant pop while BudgetDial/ModelReasoningPicker animate.
findings.proposal: Same fix as LoopStatus: AnimatePresence + motion.div with DURATION_POPOVER + EASE_DECELERATE, y:4/scale:0.98, transformOrigin "bottom left". Consider extracting one shared hover-popover wrapper since GoalStrip, LoopStatus, and BudgetDial triplicate the coords/hover-bridge/portal scaffolding.

findings.current: LoopDetailModal renders scrim + dialog with no animation at all — `if (!loop) return null` then a static portal. Every other modal surface in the app gets an entrance; this one hard-cuts in and out, including the backdrop-blur scrim.
findings.proposal: AnimatePresence around the portal content: scrim fades opacity, dialog enters with SPRING_MODAL (ENTRY_PANEL) + opacity/scale ~0.97, exit dissolving faster (opacity + scale 0.98). Matches rule 5's entrance/exit asymmetry and the app's modal language.

findings.current: Several composer sub-sections mount/unmount with no transition while their sibling QueueCard animates a rise/sink in the same bottom stack: the editing banner (324-337), pending-image strip (338-358), attached-skill pill (371-381), and GoalProposalCard (300-302, 515-554) all pop in/out and abruptly change composer height.
findings.proposal: Give each the section-entrance vocabulary: opacity + y:6-8 + blur(2px) rising into focus on mount, faster dissolving exit (opacity + scale 0.97), via AnimatePresence. Heights still snap (acceptable — the ResizeObserver already tracks --chat-bottom-h), but the content itself stops hard-cutting.

findings.current: TodoMessage rows are keyed by `${item.status}-${index}-${item.content}` (line 564), so a status flip (pending→in_progress→completed) remounts the entire <li> and the TodoIcon swaps with a hard cut — a co-located state swap with no transition, in a card whose siblings animate.
findings.proposal: Key rows by index/content only and wrap the TodoIcon in BlurSwap keyed on item.status (blur 3 for the small glyph), letting the text color/line-through change via a transition-colors token.

findings.current: ToolMessage output preview: `{!isRunning && <pre …>}` pops in instantly (with up to 80px of height) the moment the tool result lands, while the tool line itself gets a graceful shine-exit color fade (styles.css 'Beat 2/3').
findings.proposal: Fade the pre in to match the shine resolution: opacity 0→1 (optionally blur(2px)→0) over var(--duration-panel), e.g. animate-fade-in or a one-line motion wrapper with initial:false guarding replay. Keeps the height snap but removes the hard content cut.

findings.current: Send/stop glyph swap hand-rolls the BlurSwap recipe — sync AnimatePresence + col-start-1 row-start-1 grid stack + opacity/blur(4px) crossfade at MOTION.palette/EASE_OUT — adding rotate ±18° and scale 0.92 on top.
findings.proposal: Consolidate: extend BlurSwap with optional rotate/scale props (or a `variant="spin"`) and use it here, so there is exactly one overlapping-crossfade implementation. The rotate flourish is good taste — keep it, just host it in the primitive.

findings.current: Send-ack flash: useTimeoutFlag(280) removes data-just-sent after 280ms, but the ::after runs `animation: composerJustSent var(--duration-route) ease-out forwards` = 360ms — the overlay is unmounted at ~78% of its own fade, hard-cutting the tail (and 280 is an off-scale inline ms literal).
findings.proposal: Drive both from one token: hold the flag for MOTION.route * 1000 (import the token rather than a literal 280), or shorten the CSS animation to var(--duration-panel) and hold 220ms. One source of truth, no mid-animation unmount.

findings.current: use-stick-to-bottom resize spring is an inline literal: `resize: { damping: 0.92, stiffness: 0.025, mass: 1.5 }`. Library-normalized units, so SPRING_* tokens don't apply, but the values are invisible to the token system.
findings.proposal: Name it in motion.ts (e.g. `export const SPRING_SCROLL_RIVER = { damping: 0.92, stiffness: 0.025, mass: 1.5 }` with a comment that units are use-stick-to-bottom-normalized, not framer-motion) and import it, so scroll-feel tuning lives with the other motion decisions.

findings.current: The two message-entry animations used by Message.tsx are defined with raw values: `--animate-roll-in: rollIn 160ms cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1)` (160ms is off the 100/120/140/180/200/220/360 token scale) and `--animate-fade-in: fadeIn 200ms ease-out` (literal 200ms + generic ease-out instead of var(--duration-trace)/var(--ease-out-soft)).
findings.proposal: Rewrite as `rollIn var(--duration-row) var(--ease-out-soft)` (or palette if 140ms reads too quick) and `fadeIn var(--duration-trace) var(--ease-out-soft)` so the entry timings ride the token scale. Optional: add 2px blur to the keyframes to align entrances with the target vocabulary.

findings.current: Three hand-rolled hover popovers in the same composer toolbar use three different hide-bridge delays: LoopStatus 80ms, GoalStrip 120ms, BudgetDial 200ms (BudgetDial's comment justifies 200 vs 80; GoalStrip's 120 is an unexplained third value). All inline literals.
findings.proposal: Extract one useHoverPopover hook (show/scheduleHide/coords/portal) with a single named delay constant — either one value for the toolbar or a documented near/far pair. Kills the drift and the triplicated scaffolding at once.

findings.current: Raw <button>s that bypass IconButton/Chip (which carry active:scale-[0.97] duration-check) have hover-only feedback: attach-image button (446), pending-image remove X (347), edit-banner cancel (327), and GoalProposalCard's accept/edit/cancel circles (524-550) — the accept button visually mimics the send button but lacks its active:scale-[0.92].
findings.proposal: Replace the attach button and goal-proposal circles with IconButton where styling allows, or add `active:scale-[0.94] transition-[…,transform] duration-check ease-out` to match the send button's press language.

findings.current: Jump-to-bottom motion.button has no whileTap or :active scale in either variant (the unread pill also uses bare `transition-opacity` with Tailwind's default 150ms — off the token scale).
findings.proposal: Add whileTap={{ scale: 0.96 }} with SPRING_TAP (it's already a motion.button), and give the pill variant `duration-fast` like its chevron sibling.

findings.current: ModelReasoningPicker trigger chip is hover-only (`transition-colors`, no active scale), while every neighboring pressable in the toolbar (Chip, IconButton, EffortPill, BudgetDial trigger) presses at active:scale-[0.97] duration-check.
findings.proposal: Match the toolbar convention: `transition-[background-color,color,transform] duration-check ease-out active:scale-[0.97]`.

findings.current: Queue-row cancel X: `transition-colors` hover only, no active scale, and no duration token (default 150ms).
findings.proposal: Add `active:scale-[0.92] transition-[color,background-color,transform] duration-check ease-out` (the recipe LoopStatus's stop button already uses), or swap to IconButton size xs.

findings.current: Chat-core leans on bare `transition-colors` / `transition-opacity` in many spots (Chat.tsx 63/68, Composer.tsx 330/351/376/451, Message.tsx 215/357, LoopStatus.tsx 162/169, ComposerSelectors.tsx 120, QueueCard.tsx 82/100…) which all resolve to Tailwind's default 150ms — a duration that exists nowhere on the 100/120/140/180/200/220/360 token scale.
findings.proposal: Register `--default-transition-duration: var(--duration-row)` (140ms) and `--default-transition-timing-function: var(--ease-out-soft)` in the @theme block so every bare transition-* utility lands on the token scale, instead of chasing call sites.

vocabulary: Token fluency is high here: modal scrims fade at MOTION.trace + EASE_DECELERATE and panels land with ENTRY_PANEL (scale ~0.96, y 6), while the trace itself speaks a ticker dialect — rows spring in with opacity + y(8) and exit upward (opacity + y:-8) under popLayout, paired with RollingToken odometer slides (MOTION.palette, EASE_EMPHASIZED) for header text; notably row entrances use no blur, diverging from the app-wide blur-rise entrance vocabulary. Co-located swaps consistently route through BlurSwap at MOTION.check (CopyGlyph everywhere, Mermaid's fullscreen icon) — the primitive is well adopted except for adjacent text labels. CSS-side hover/press feedback runs on --duration-check/--duration-row but with generic `ease`/`ease-out` instead of the --ease-* tokens, and ambient loops (tool-line shine 2.2s, streaming-code sheen 2.4s) animate background-position under background-clip:text (paint-bound, deliberate). The single off-token spring is the rolling trace-row spring (350/40/0.8), a near-duplicate of SPRING_ROW_ENTRY (360/28/0.8).

findings.current: transition={ suppressMotion ? { duration: 0 } : { type: "spring", stiffness: 350, damping: 40, mass: 0.8 } } — raw spring literal driving enter/exit/layout for every rolling trace row, the most-fired animation in the trace. It is a near-duplicate of SPRING_ROW_ENTRY (360/28/0.8) in motion.ts but with damping 40 (no overshoot), so the drift is invisible.
findings.proposal: Promote to a named token in apps/desktop/src/lib/tokens/motion.ts (e.g. SPRING_TRACE_ROW = { type: "spring", stiffness: 350, damping: 40, mass: 0.8 }) with a comment distinguishing it from SPRING_ROW_ENTRY, or reuse SPRING_ROW_ENTRY if the heavier damping isn't load-bearing. Import it here.

findings.current: Workflow cards mount in a plain <div key={`wf:...`} className="mt-1 space-y-1"> outside the AnimatePresence, and ExpandableWorkflowCard/WorkflowProgressCard has no entrance of its own — so when a workflow lands mid-run it pops in instantly while sibling tool rows around it spring in with opacity + y.
findings.proposal: Wrap the card wrapper in a motion.div with the same row entrance treatment (initial={suppressMotion ? false : { opacity: 0, y: 8 }}, animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}, transition = the trace-row spring token), optionally with filter: blur(2px) → 0 per the section-entrance vocabulary. Guard with suppressMotion like the rows.

findings.current: Static (post-run) collapse animates height: animate={{ opacity: collapsed ? 0 : 1, height: collapsed ? 0 : `${visible.length * ROW_HEIGHT_EM}em` }} with SPRING_LAYOUT — a spring-driven height reflow inside the same stick-to-bottom scroller whose resize-spring chasing this very file documents (rolling-mode comment, lines 252-258).
findings.proposal: Acceptable as a one-shot user toggle with exact em height (no auto-measure), but it's the only height animation in the area. If frame timing shows the scroll container chasing it, fall back to instant height + per-row opacity/blur fade; otherwise keep and note the exception explicitly where the motion rules live.

findings.current: Bare Tailwind transition utilities ride the unthemed default 150ms + default ease, which is off the --duration-* scale (140ms row / 180ms palette bracket it): `transition-opacity` (L537 agent glyph, L558 stop tooltip, L600 ArrowUpRight), `transition-[opacity,color]` (L553 stop button), `transition-colors` (L580 agent name). L211's chevron has `duration-trace` but no ease token (defaults to Tailwind's ease).
findings.proposal: Append `duration-row ease-out` (the hover tier used by .app-row/IconButton) to each, or set `--default-transition-duration: var(--duration-row)` in the @theme block so bare utilities snap to the scale app-wide.

findings.current: Both copy buttons use bare `transition-colors` — Tailwind default 150ms/default ease, off the token scale (same drift class as the ActivityTrace finding).
findings.proposal: Add `duration-row ease-out` (or rely on a themed --default-transition-duration). Note these two buttons are byte-identical implementations within one file — extract a shared CopyChip while touching them.

findings.current: {copied ? "Copied" : "Copy"} — the text label hard-cuts (and changes width) while the adjacent CopyGlyph blur-crossfades, so half the control animates and half snaps. Co-located label swap in one slot.
findings.proposal: Wrap the label in BlurSwap (swapKey={copied ? "copied" : "copy"}, blur 2-3) to match the glyph; BlurSwap's grid-stack sizes to the larger child so the width jump also disappears.

findings.current: The Result slot swaps between four co-located states (error / "Loading result…" / "Working…" / Markdown result) with a hard cut. With the viewer open while an agent finishes, "Working…" snaps into the full result block with no transition — a status-text swap per the BlurSwap rule.
findings.proposal: Key the slot's content by state and wrap in BlurSwap (or give just the arriving result block a one-shot opacity + blur(2px) entrance at MOTION.trace/EASE_DECELERATE). One-shot per view, not stream-frequency.

findings.current: Hand-rolled modal scaffolding (scrim fade MOTION.trace/EASE_DECELERATE + panel ENTRY_PANEL at scale 0.96/y 6) duplicates PageModal, which uses scale 0.95 — a small pattern drift between the app's two modal implementations. Mermaid.tsx (105-118) carries the same 0.96 literal set.
findings.proposal: Render ToolViewer through PageModal (MarkdownViewer already does), or extract the initial/exit pose ({ opacity: 0, scale: 0.96, y: 6 }) into a shared constant next to ENTRY_PANEL so the modal pose can't drift per call site.

findings.current: The two copy chips have hover color/bg states but no press feedback (no :active scale, no whileTap). Batch note: ActivityTrace's header toggle (ActivityTrace.tsx L179-216) also lacks press feedback, while sibling controls (IconButton, .app-row rows, Mermaid's toolbar) all carry active:scale ~0.97/0.985.
findings.proposal: Add `active:scale-[0.97]` to both chips (matches IconButton's language; their transition list already needs the duration token fix). For the trace header toggle, an opacity/color press state fits better than scale on a bare text line.

findings.current: .md .code-block-copy.copied plays `codeCopyFlash` — scale 0.92 → 1.04 → 1 overshoot bounce plus accent bg flash — layered on top of CopyGlyph's blur crossfade, making it a two-stage ack. No other copy button in the area flashes (ToolViewer's and Mermaid's acks are color + crossfade only).
findings.proposal: Delete the codeCopyFlash keyframe and the animation line; keep the color/bg .copied state + CopyGlyph crossfade. If press feedback is wanted instead, add :active { transform: scale(0.97) } consistent with IconButton.

findings.current: The "Rendering…" dashed placeholder (~40px) hard-cuts into the 480px MermaidPanel the instant the SVG resolves (same for the error block swap) — a jarring section mount with zero transition, in a file that otherwise animates everything through tokens.
findings.proposal: Give MermaidPanel's root a one-shot mount entrance per the section vocabulary: opacity 0 + blur(3px) + y 8 → settled, MOTION.trace + EASE_DECELERATE (motion.div, initial only — no exit needed). The height jump remains but the mount stops flashing.

findings.current: MermaidErrorBlock's copy button hand-rolls IconButton's exact motion chrome (`transition-[background-color,color,transform] duration-check ease-out ... active:scale-[0.97]`) as a one-off with bad-tinted colors. Timing is token-correct today but will silently drift if IconButton's feedback changes.
findings.proposal: Use IconButton (it already has a `danger` tone) with className overrides for the border/bad tint, so press/hover timing stays single-sourced.

vocabulary: This area is largely tokenized: roster lists (todos, agents, workflows) enter with opacity + y(-4) at MOTION.row/EASE_OUT and exit with opacity + scale 0.97 at MOTION.fast under AnimatePresence popLayout with SPRING_ROW_ENTRY layout springs — note exits omit the blur(~4px) from the stated list-exit vocabulary, a mild area-wide drift. Every expand/collapse is one copy-pasted idiom: animated gridTemplateRows 0fr→1fr at MOTION.panel + EASE_EMPHASIZED (5 sites: row composer, deny-reason, breadcrumb, workflow source, phase groups) — a non-GPU height tween that should be a shared primitive. Icon swaps correctly route through BlurSwap (busy spinner, CopyGlyph), and modals use ENTRY_PANEL springs with origin-aware transforms. The split personality is ApprovalBanner: it is the only spring-physics surface here and defines its own local spring (340/32/0.9) plus raw 0.16s/"easeOut" opacity timing, while hover/color transitions across the area alternate between tokenized duration-row/duration-check ease-out and bare Tailwind defaults (150ms, cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1)) that sit off the 140/180ms token scale.

findings.current: Local spring defined inline: `const SPRING = { type: "spring", stiffness: 340, damping: 32, mass: 0.9 }` (line 13), plus raw timing in the card transition: `opacity: { duration: 0.16, ease: "easeOut" }` (line 210). Neither comes from lib/tokens/motion.ts.
findings.proposal: Replace SPRING with SPRING_MODAL (380/32/1 — perceptually identical confident settle) or promote the 340/32/0.9 tune to a named token (e.g. SPRING_STACK) if it's load-bearing. Replace the opacity literal with `{ duration: MOTION.row, ease: EASE_OUT }` (0.14s) or MOTION.palette if 0.16 was deliberate — either way it must live on the scale so drift stays visible.

findings.current: Deny-with-reason reveal animates `gridTemplateRows: "0fr" → "1fr"` (per-frame layout) at MOTION.panel/EASE_OUT. One of 5 identical copy-pasted grid-rows collapses in this area.
findings.proposal: The revealed content is a single fixed-height row (h-7 input + button), the cheapest possible case — but it's still a layout-thrashing height tween inside a spring-animated card stack. Extract ONE shared <Collapse> primitive for all 5 sites, measure frame timing (project memory: grid-rows reveals shipped jank twice), and for this fixed-height case prefer animating the container height via FLIP/layout spring with the row entering on opacity+blur.

findings.current: `paddingTop: approvals.length > 1 ? 14 : 0` is an unanimated inline style — when a second approval arrives or the stack drains to one, the front card jumps 14px instantly while the sliver cards spring smoothly around it.
findings.proposal: Always reserve the 14px headroom (the stack sits above the composer; constant padding costs nothing visually), or let a layout animation absorb the shift. Constant padding is the simpler, more elegant fix.

findings.current: Inline send-composer reveal animates `gridTemplateRows: "0fr" → "1fr"` at MOTION.panel/EASE_EMPHASIZED — per-frame layout inside the dense sidebar roster, reflowing every row below it on every frame.
findings.proposal: Same shared <Collapse> consolidation as the other 4 sites. Content is one fixed-height input row (h-7), so a FLIP/layout-spring height change with the input entering on opacity+blur(2px) is viable; at minimum measure frame timing with a full roster before keeping the grid-rows tween.

findings.current: The hover swap glyph→stop-button is an overlapping opacity crossfade, but its two halves run on different clocks: the Bot glyph fades out via bare `transition-opacity` (Tailwind default 150ms, cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1)) while the stop button fades in via `duration-row ease-out` (140ms).
findings.proposal: Add `duration-row ease-out` to the glyph span's `transition-opacity` (line 77) so both halves of the crossfade share one timing.

findings.current: WorkflowSource disclosure animates `gridTemplateRows: "0fr" → "1fr"` over a syntax-highlighted `<pre>` up to 40vh tall (`max-h-[40vh]`, dangerouslySetInnerHTML) — the heaviest of the 5 grid-rows collapses; every frame re-layouts the entire highlighted code block plus everything below it in the sidebar.
findings.proposal: Worst candidate for a height tween. Mount the pre with opacity + blur(2px) only (no height animation — the source block sits at the bottom of the expanded card so the jump is contained), or if a reveal is wanted, FLIP the container with the pre wrapped in `contain: layout paint`. Fold into the shared <Collapse> primitive with a 'heavy content' escape hatch.

findings.current: PhaseGroup expand/collapse animates `gridTemplateRows: "0fr" → "1fr"` at MOTION.panel/EASE_EMPHASIZED over N agent rows — per-frame layout that also reflows sibling phases and roster rows below.
findings.proposal: Consolidate into the shared <Collapse> primitive and measure with a many-agent phase; the agent rows could enter as a quick opacity+y stagger with the height change FLIP-driven by the parent layout spring already present on the sidebar's popLayout wrappers.

findings.current: ExpandableWorkflowCard: `{expanded && (<><WorkflowDetail …/><WorkflowSource …/></>)}` — the whole phases→agents detail mounts/unmounts with zero transition. The card height jumps instantly while sibling rows in the hub animate with SPRING_ROW_ENTRY popLayout, and the inner PhaseGroups DO animate their own collapses — so the outer expand is the only hard cut in the chain.
findings.proposal: Wrap in AnimatePresence: enter opacity 0→1 + y(-4→0) + blur(2px→0) at MOTION.row/EASE_OUT, exit faster dissolve (opacity + scale 0.97 at MOTION.fast), matching the area's row vocabulary. In the sidebar the existing `layout` wrapper will FLIP the sibling reflow; the chat-trace instance needs the entrance most.

findings.current: The Copy button only mounts while the disclosure is open (`{open && …}`) and pops in with no transition; inside it, the `{copied ? "Copied" : "Copy"}` label hard-swaps while the adjacent CopyGlyph icon blur-crossfades via BlurSwap — half the button animates, half snaps.
findings.proposal: Wrap the label in the already-imported-adjacent BlurSwap (`swapKey={copied ? "copied" : "copy"}`, blur 3) so icon and label crossfade together; give the button a quick opacity fade on mount (MOTION.check) so it doesn't pop next to the rotating chevron.

findings.current: ParentBreadcrumb reveal animates `gridTemplateRows: "0fr" → "1fr"` at MOTION.panel/EASE_EMPHASIZED — per-frame layout at the very top of the panel, reflowing the entire roster below it for the whole 220ms.
findings.proposal: Same shared <Collapse> consolidation; the breadcrumb is one fixed-height row, so FLIP the height via the panel's layout context and enter the row with opacity+blur. Measure: this fires on every parent↔child session switch, a frequent navigation.

findings.current: ApprovalsRow — the single load-bearing 'needs you' amber signal — returns null/mounts with zero transition (`if (count === 0 || !firstToolId) return null`), while its direct siblings (breadcrumb above, agent rows below) all animate in and out.
findings.proposal: Give it the area's entrance: AnimatePresence with opacity + y(-4) + blur(2px) in at MOTION.row/EASE_OUT, faster dissolve out (opacity + scale 0.97, MOTION.fast). Approvals arrive at most a few times an hour — this is exactly the attention-worthy mount the vocabulary is for, no decoration needed.

findings.current: Automation rows render as a plain `.map` with no AnimatePresence/motion — a running automation pops into and out of the hub instantly, while the Agents and Workflows sections directly above animate identical rows with popLayout + SPRING_ROW_ENTRY + opacity/y/scale.
findings.proposal: Wrap in the same `<AnimatePresence initial={false} mode="popLayout">` + motion.div recipe used at lines 798-820 (layout: SPRING_ROW_ENTRY, opacity/y at MOTION.row, exit scale 0.97 at MOTION.fast) — it's the same AgentRunRow body, so the three sections should share one list-motion treatment (worth extracting the wrapper given it's now needed 3x).

findings.current: The 'New task…' add-row (`{adding && (<div …>)}`) pops in with no transition directly beneath todo rows that animate via popLayout spring.
findings.proposal: Mount it with the todo-row entrance (opacity 0→1, y -4→0 at MOTION.row/EASE_OUT); no exit needed — commit/cancel replaces it with a real row or removes focus, both already handled.

findings.current: All status changes on the workflow card hard-cut via class swaps: agent pips (pipClass), phase bar segments (phaseSegmentClass), the glyph tone (accent→muted), the name color, and the Badge tone + word ('running'→'completed'). Meanwhile StatusDot — the same status vocabulary one section up — deliberately crossfades its color change.
findings.proposal: Add `transition-colors duration-trace ease-out` to the pip/segment/glyph spans so status flips crossfade like StatusDot does, and wrap the Badge content in BlurSwap keyed by status for the word swap. Status changes happen a handful of times per run — cheap, consistent, no ornament.

findings.current: ~18 bare `transition-colors`/`transition-opacity`/`transition-transform` utilities across the area run at Tailwind's untokenized default: 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1) — off the token scale (between --duration-row 140ms and --duration-palette 180ms) and a symmetric ease, not the app's ease-out language. Siblings in the same files DO tokenize (e.g. AgentRunRow 95/158 use duration-row/duration-check). Worst case: WorkflowProgress.tsx 212's chevron rotation (a transform) at the default symmetric ease, while the equivalent chevrons in WorkflowDetail 98/212 use `duration-row`.
findings.proposal: Sweep: append `duration-row ease-out` for hover color/opacity shifts and `duration-row` to the WorkflowProgress chevron's transition-transform. Alternatively set `--default-transition-duration: var(--duration-row)` and `--default-transition-timing-function` once in @theme so bare utilities land on-scale by default — the more elegant, drift-proof fix.

findings.current: The global press rule `.sidebar button:not(:disabled):active { scale: 0.96 }` (styles.css) is scoped to the LEFT rail only; the right agent hub's aside uses `surface-panel` so none of its pressables get press feedback — ApprovalsRow, ParentBreadcrumb, todo add/cycle/edit buttons, WorkflowProgressCard, its stop control, agent/source/phase rows, and the hover-lane ActionButtons all press dead, while the structurally-mirrored left sidebar's buttons all compress.
findings.proposal: Extract the `.sidebar button` press rule into a shared class (e.g. `.pressable-chrome`, same `scale var(--duration-check)` transition + `:active { scale: 0.96 }`) and apply it to both rails' roots. Batch fix — one class, whole panel covered, no per-button churn.

findings.current: The modal's footer Reject/Approve buttons have only `transition-colors`/`transition-opacity` hover states — no `active:scale` — while the IDENTICAL Approve/Reject actions on the ApprovalBanner footer (ApprovalBanner.tsx 366-445) all carry `active:scale-[0.97] duration-check ease-out`. Same action, two surfaces, only one presses.
findings.proposal: Copy the banner's recipe onto both buttons: `transition-[background-color,border-color,color,transform] duration-check ease-out active:scale-[0.97]` — one consistent press language for the approve/reject pair wherever it appears.

vocabulary: This area is solidly token-driven at the surface level: the editor modal enters on ENTRY_PANEL (SPRING_MODAL) with opacity+scale 0.96+y8, the schedule popover on SPRING_POPOVER with a MOTION.fast/EASE_OUT exit, card grids use SPRING_LAYOUT with popLayout, and sidebar reveals/empty states use MOTION.panel + EASE_EMPHASIZED; micro-interactions ride shared CSS primitives (app-row, IconButton, Chip, RowAction) at duration-check ease-out with active:scale-0.97. Drift shows at the edges: bare Tailwind `transition-colors`/`transition-opacity` (default 150ms generic ease, vs the 140ms duration-row token) is the de facto hover idiom (~83 bare vs 6 tokenized app-wide), and generic Tailwind `ease-out` stands in for --ease-out-soft on micro-transitions — both are house-wide patterns, not flagged per instance. Popover language is split: ScheduleChip springs in while SidebarFilters and SessionContextMenu pop in/out with a position-ready opacity toggle and no exit. Exits reuse the entrance spring (no faster dissolve, no blur), and the sidebar's collapse reveals still animate grid-template-rows — the one known-jank pattern surviving in this area.

findings.current: Group collapse and the "show more" reveal both tween grid-template-rows via framer-motion: initial={{ gridTemplateRows: "0fr", opacity: 0 }} → animate "1fr" with { duration: MOTION.panel, ease: EASE_EMPHASIZED }. Animating grid-template-rows relayouts every session row (plus the scroll container) on each frame — this is the exact grid-rows-reveal pattern that previously shipped jank.
findings.proposal: Drop the height tween. Linear-style: snap the height (mount/unmount rows instantly) and animate only opacity + a small -y on the row block (MOTION.row, EASE_OUT) — or clip the reveal with an animated clip-path inset on the wrapper if a masked reveal is wanted. Either keeps the transition on compositor props. Apply the same fix to both the group collapse (201) and the expanded-overflow block (213).

findings.current: The filter/group popover renders with style={{ opacity: pos.ready ? 1 : 0 }} — it pops to full opacity once positioned and unmounts instantly on close. No entrance, no exit, while the sibling popover in this same area (ScheduleChip in AutomationEditor) springs in on SPRING_POPOVER with a MOTION.fast/EASE_OUT exit.
findings.proposal: Wrap the portal content in AnimatePresence + motion.div using the established popover vocabulary: initial {opacity:0, scale:0.97, y:-4}, animate with SPRING_POPOVER, exit {opacity:0, scale:0.97} over MOTION.fast/EASE_OUT, transformOrigin top-right (it opens below/right-aligned to the trigger). Keep the position-ready gate for the first frame.

findings.current: Context menu uses the same opacity:pos.ready?1:0 pattern — instant pop in, instant unmount. No AnimatePresence.
findings.proposal: Match the app's popover vocabulary: SPRING_POPOVER entrance ({opacity:0, scale:0.97, y:-2}, transformOrigin from the clamped anchor corner) + MOTION.fast/EASE_OUT exit via AnimatePresence (requires lifting the conditional render in SessionList so exit can play). If native-instant-open is the deliberate taste for right-click menus, at minimum add the fast dissolving exit so dismissal isn't a hard cut.

findings.current: The leading 16px state glyph hard-swaps between four co-located states (pulsing StatusDot ↔ unread dot ↔ Radio ↔ empty) with plain conditional returns — no transition. The streaming→unread swap fires exactly when a run completes, the moment the user is watching the row.
findings.proposal: Wrap the slot in the shared BlurSwap keyed on the state name ("streaming" | "unread" | "channel" | "agent" | "idle") with blur={2} — tiny glyphs need the small radius per BlurSwap's own doc. Co-located icon swaps are exactly what BlurSwap exists for.

findings.current: const MODAL_BACKDROP_DURATION = 0.2; — a local literal used for the scrim fade (transition={{ duration: MODAL_BACKDROP_DURATION, ease: EASE_DECELERATE }}). PageModal.tsx already defines the same value as BACKDROP_DURATION = MOTION.trace; this re-declares 0.2 raw, so the scrim timing can silently drift from the rest of the modals.
findings.proposal: Replace with MOTION.trace from lib/tokens/motion (delete the local constant), matching PageModal's scrim.

findings.current: fieldStackCls = (active) => clsx("[grid-area:1/1] grid gap-2.5 content-start", active ? "opacity-100 transition-opacity duration-row ease-out" : "invisible opacity-0") — the trigger-kind panels are grid-stacked in one slot (good), but only the ACTIVE state carries the transition class. Switching kinds drops the outgoing panel to invisible/opacity-0 instantly while the incoming one fades in over 140ms, so the slot is near-empty for the first half of the swap — an asymmetric, effectively empty-frame crossfade.
findings.proposal: Make the crossfade overlap: keep "transition-opacity duration-row ease-out" on BOTH states and replace `invisible` with `pointer-events-none` plus a delayed visibility flip ([transition:opacity_var(--duration-row)_var(--ease-out-soft),visibility_0s_var(--duration-row)]) so the outgoing panel dissolves while the incoming rises — the same sync-overlap principle as BlurSwap, kept in CSS since the panels must stay mounted for height stability.

findings.current: The three conditional banners (unsafe auto-approve warning, message-trigger info, save error) mount/unmount with no transition inside an otherwise spring-animated modal — toggling Auto-Approve or hitting a save error makes a full-width banner pop in and the footer/modal height snap.
findings.proposal: Wrap each in AnimatePresence with the house entrance vocabulary: initial {opacity:0, y:6, filter:"blur(3px)"} → animate clear over MOTION.panel/EASE_DECELERATE; exit faster and dissolving ({opacity:0, scale:0.97, filter:"blur(3px)"}, MOTION.row/EASE_OUT). Let the height snap (animating it would break the GPU rule) — the fade covers the reflow.

findings.current: {saving ? "Saving…" : seed.kind === "edit" ? "Save" : "Create"} — the primary button's label is a co-located text swap that hard-pops, and the width difference between "Create"/"Saving…" makes the button jump.
findings.proposal: Wrap the label in BlurSwap (swapKey = saving ? "saving" : kind, blur 2–3) — the file already imports and uses BlurSwap for the schedule chip icons. Optionally pin a min-width so the width doesn't jump mid-swap.

findings.current: SuggestionCard and AutomationCard both exit with {opacity:0, scale:0.97} driven by the entrance spring (SPRING_LAYOUT, stiffness 220 — the slowest spring in the set). House vocabulary for list-row exits is opacity + scale 0.96 + blur(~4px), with exits faster than entrances; here the dismissal/deletion exit is as slow as the entrance and has no blur.
findings.proposal: Give both cards an explicit exit transition: exit={{ opacity:0, scale:0.96, filter:"blur(4px)", transition:{ duration: MOTION.row, ease: EASE_OUT } }}, keeping SPRING_LAYOUT for layout/entrance only.

findings.current: SuggestionsSection returns null until the async fetchAutomationSuggestions resolves, then mounts a whole section above the static templates — heading + cards pop in and shove the template grid down with no transition (the inner AnimatePresence has initial={false}, so card entrances are suppressed on first arrival too).
findings.proposal: Animate the late arrival: give the section wrapper an entrance (opacity + y 8 + blur(3px), MOTION.panel/EASE_DECELERATE) when suggestions transition from empty→non-empty, or let the cards' existing initial {opacity:0, scale:0.98} play on first data arrival instead of suppressing it (key the AnimatePresence so initial={false} only applies to tab revisits).

findings.current: Batch: the header "New" button (100), the empty-state "Browse templates"/"Start from scratch" buttons (201-214), the SuggestionCard stretched click target (374), the AutomationCard stretched click target (614), and TemplateCard (654) are all raw <button>s with no whileTap/:active scale. (IconButton/Chip/app-row consumers in this area already have it via shared CSS.)
findings.proposal: Add active:scale-[0.97] (with transform in the transition list, duration-check ease-out) to the buttons; for the full-card targets apply a gentler active:scale-[0.99] on the card article/div (the stretched button can't scale its parent — put the :active selector on the group via has-[button:active]:scale-[0.99] or move cards to motion.article whileTap).

findings.current: Editor footer Cancel and Create/Save buttons have hover transitions (transition-colors / transition-opacity) but no whileTap or :active scale — the modal's primary commit action gives no press acknowledgment.
findings.proposal: Add active:scale-[0.97] and include transform in the transition (transition-[color,opacity,transform] duration-check ease-out), matching IconButton/Chip.

findings.current: Every interactive element in this modal lacks transitions entirely: inputs/textarea flip focus:border-accent instantly (the editor's schedFieldCls animates border-color/box-shadow), and the Choose/Cancel/Save buttons have hover:bg/hover:text styles with no transition class and no press feedback — instant state flips inside an app where every sibling surface transitions at duration-check/duration-row.
findings.proposal: Bring it in line with the area's idiom: add transition-[border-color,box-shadow] to the fields (plus the focus shadow ring used by schedFieldCls), transition-colors + active:scale-[0.97] (transform in the list, duration-check ease-out) to the three buttons.

vocabulary: Settings tabs are almost framer-free: motion lives in shared primitives (Tabs pill indicator = SPRING_LAYOUT, TabPanels = MOTION.palette + EASE_EMPHASIZED directional slide, IconButton = duration-check + active:scale-[0.97], SegmentedControl/SwitchControl = CSS --duration-panel/--ease-out-soft), while tab content itself uses bare Tailwind transition-colors/opacity hover utilities (stock 150ms default, occasionally duration-check/row) and animate-spin + instant text swaps for busy states. The memory pane is much richer: SPRING_LAYOUT for accordions/chevrons/layout, SPRING_POPOVER/SPRING_MODAL/SPRING_CARD for panel entrances, BlurSwap for status glyphs, popLayout lists with MOTION.fast/row durations, and skeleton shimmer for loading. The drift pattern is consistent: memory reaches for gridTemplateRows "0fr→1fr" reveals for every expand/collapse (the known jank pattern) and sometimes omits transition/ease entirely (framer defaults), while settings simply doesn't animate mounts at all (popovers, drill-ins, inline editors, row removals pop). Both sides time JS side-effects to animations with magic-number setTimeouts (240ms, 380ms) instead of tokens or onExitComplete.

findings.current: Three expand/collapse reveals animate gridTemplateRows: Composer `initial={{ opacity: 0, gridTemplateRows: "0fr" }} animate={{ opacity: 1, gridTemplateRows: "1fr" }}` with SPRING_LAYOUT (L108-111), and the same pattern for GroupedProfiles group bodies (L921-924) and ClaimSources (L1010-1013). grid-template-rows is a layout prop — every spring frame reflows the markdown/claim list below it; this is the exact grid-rows-reveal pattern previously identified as shipped jank.
findings.proposal: Drop the grid-rows spring. For these dense disclosure sections, collapse/expand the layout instantly and animate only the inner content into focus per the house vocabulary (motion.div with opacity 0→1, y -4→0, filter blur(2px)→0, transition { duration: MOTION.row, ease: EASE_OUT }), exits faster (MOTION.fast, opacity + blur). If a height bridge is truly wanted, FLIP it with a measured transform instead of animating grid rows.

findings.current: exitVariant animates height: 0 and marginBottom: 0 (`{ opacity: 0, x: -24, height: 0, marginBottom: 0 }` for reject, `{ opacity: 0, height: 0, marginBottom: 0 }` for supersede) — non-GPU layout props. Worse, the exit is dead code: no ancestor wraps ClaimBlock in AnimatePresence (ClaimSources in LensesView.tsx L1016-1030 maps blocks in a plain div), so on supersede/reject the block sits static for the 240ms timer window and then pops out on refetch — the direction-encoded exit never plays.
findings.proposal: Wrap the block list in `<AnimatePresence mode="popLayout" initial={false}>` in ClaimSources, keep `layout` on ClaimBlock so siblings collapse via transform, and use the house list-row exit: opacity 0 + scale 0.96 + blur(4px) (plus x: -24 for reject), transition { duration: MOTION.row, ease: EASE_OUT }. Then drive the refetch from onExitComplete instead of the 240ms timer.

findings.current: Delete-view confirm swap uses `<AnimatePresence initial={false} mode="wait">` over two co-located states sharing the actions slot: "actions" (Edit criterion / Delete view) and "confirm" (Cancel / Delete view + caption). mode="wait" finishes the exit (opacity 0, MOTION.fast) before the enter starts, leaving an empty actions row mid-transition; the "actions" branch also re-enters with initial={false} so it snaps in.
findings.proposal: Replace with the shared BlurSwap primitive (swapKey={confirmingDelete ? "confirm" : "actions"}) so the two states blur-crossfade in place with no empty frame — same grid-stack sync overlap used elsewhere; drop the hand-rolled x: 6 slide.

findings.current: CoverageMeter animates the bar with `animate={{ width: `${pct}%` }}` + `layout` under SPRING_LAYOUT — width is a layout prop and triggers reflow/repaint every spring frame.
findings.proposal: Render the fill at width 100% and animate `scaleX: coverage.ratio` with `style={{ originX: 0 }}` (transform-only), keeping SPRING_LAYOUT for the settle. Drop the redundant `layout` prop.

findings.current: The inspector panel is keyed by `selected.id` inside AnimatePresence with a SPRING_MODAL x: 28 slide. Selecting a different node while the panel is open exits the old panel (slide right) and enters a new one (slide from right) simultaneously — two absolutely-stacked panels at right-3/top-3 sliding through each other, and the full open-gesture replays on every node click.
findings.proposal: Key the motion.div statically (key="inspector") so the slide plays only on open/close, and swap the inner content keyed by selected.id with BlurSwap (or a single opacity+blur crossfade) — node-to-node selection becomes a quick in-place content swap instead of a re-slide.

findings.current: Header title `{TABS.find((t) => t.id === active)?.label}` swaps text instantly on tab change while the panel below slides directionally via TabPanels — the co-located label pops while its sibling animates.
findings.proposal: Wrap the title in the shared BlurSwap (`<BlurSwap swapKey={active}>…</BlurSwap>`, default MOTION.check) so the label blur-crossfades in step with the panel slide.

findings.current: The list ↔ add/edit drill-in swaps the entire pane with conditional returns and zero transition — ServerForm replaces ServerList instantly, inside a modal where every sibling tab switch slides via TabPanels.
findings.proposal: Give the drill-in the house entrance: wrap the active view in a keyed motion.div (opacity 0→1, y 8→0, blur(2px)→0, MOTION.palette + EASE_OUT; exit faster, opacity + slight -y). Alternatively reuse the TabPanels directional slide (forward into the form, back out to the list) since it's an ordered navigation.

findings.current: PalettePicker's portaled popover (`surface-panel surface-popover fixed z-[60]`) mounts and unmounts with no animation — it pops open/closed, while every other popover in the app (LensEvidenceSearch, command palette, composer pickers) enters with SPRING_POPOVER or --animate-pop-in.
findings.proposal: Animate it like the other popovers: AnimatePresence + motion.div with initial {opacity: 0, scale: 0.98, y: -4} → animate {1, 1, 0} under SPRING_POPOVER (transformOrigin top), exit opacity-only at MOTION.fast — or simply add the existing `--animate-pop-in` utility class. Longer term this hand-rolled dropdown duplicates the composer's picker popover; consider consolidating.

findings.current: The @theme block defines --duration-* / --ease-* tokens but never overrides Tailwind v4's --default-transition-duration / --default-transition-timing-function, so the ~51 bare `transition-colors` / `transition-opacity` / `transition-[…]` utilities across settings/ and memory/ (e.g. ArchiveTab.tsx:96, ConnectionTab.tsx:49, ProvidersTab.tsx:430, ClaimsView.tsx:200, LensEvidenceSearch.tsx:220) run at the stock 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1) while siblings that specify duration-check/duration-row run at 120/140ms with ease-out — two timing languages on adjacent controls.
findings.proposal: Set `--default-transition-duration: var(--duration-row)` and `--default-transition-timing-function: var(--ease-out-soft)` in @theme so every bare transition utility inherits the token scale; no per-callsite churn needed.

findings.current: `exitTimerRef.current = window.setTimeout(() => { … }, 240)` — a hardcoded 240ms window meant to let the claim exit animation play before refetch. The value corresponds to no token (MOTION.trace = 200ms, MOTION.panel = 220ms) and, since the exit never actually plays (see ClaimBlock finding), it's pure dead air.
findings.proposal: Once the exit runs under AnimatePresence, replace the timer with `onExitComplete` so the refetch is animation-driven; if a timer must stay, derive it from the token (`MOTION.trace * 1000`).

findings.current: The runNote toast (motion.span, initial {opacity: 0, y: 4} → animate, exit {opacity: 0}) specifies NO transition at all — it rides framer-motion's untokened defaults (~0.3s tween / default spring). GenerationProgress (L1105) and its detail span (L1146) set token durations (MOTION.panel / MOTION.fast) but omit ease, falling back to framer's default curve.
findings.proposal: Add explicit token transitions: runNote `transition={{ duration: MOTION.row, ease: EASE_OUT }}`; add `ease: EASE_OUT` to the GenerationProgress entrance and detail fade so nothing in the file uses library-default timing.

findings.current: The ease-pan uses the CSS token (`transition: "transform var(--duration-route) var(--ease-emphasized)"`, L351 — good) but the JS reset timer is a hardcoded `setTimeout(() => setEasePan(false), 380)` while --duration-route is 360ms; the 380 literal will silently drift if the token changes.
findings.proposal: Derive the timer from the token: `MOTION.route * 1000 + 20` (import from lib/tokens/motion), or listen for `transitionend` on the <g> instead of a parallel timer.

findings.current: Hover/selection focus snaps the whole graph between lit and dimmed states instantly — node groups jump opacity 1 ↔ 0.18 and links 0.4/0.85 ↔ 0.06 with no transition, so moving the pointer across nodes flickers the entire canvas.
findings.proposal: Add `transition: opacity var(--duration-row) ease-out` (CSS class on the node <g> and link <line> elements — opacity only, compositor-friendly, unaffected by sim-tick re-renders) so the spotlight dims/lights smoothly.

findings.current: ClaimDetail animates only the header block in (motion.div opacity 0/y 4 under SPRING_CARD) while the stats grid, sections, meta, and actions below mount with no transition — the body pops while its sibling rises.
findings.proposal: Lift the entrance one level: animate a single wrapper around the whole DetailShell content (same opacity + y 4 + optional blur(2px), SPRING_CARD or MOTION.panel + EASE_OUT) so the detail rises as one unit; drop the header-only motion.div.

findings.current: On close, the "Find entries" GhostBtn (`{!open && …}`, outside the AnimatePresence) re-renders instantly above the panel that is still playing its SPRING_POPOVER exit — both occupy the slot briefly, then the layout jumps up when the exit completes.
findings.proposal: Move the trigger inside the same swap: either gate it on exit completion (AnimatePresence onExitComplete) or give it a matching keyed motion.span (opacity 0→1 at MOTION.check) so the button fades in as the panel dissolves instead of popping mid-exit.

findings.current: Restore/delete removes an ArchivedRow with no exit — the row pops out and the list reflows instantly, while the app's other mutable lists (LensEvidenceSearch results) animate with popLayout.
findings.proposal: Wrap the rows in `<AnimatePresence mode="popLayout" initial={false}>` with `layout` rows and the house list-row exit (opacity 0 + scale 0.96 + blur(4px), MOTION.row + EASE_OUT, layout: SPRING_LAYOUT) — matches the LensEvidenceSearch precedent (which also tolerates it during live filtering).

findings.current: The inline API-key editor slab (`{editing && !provider.connected && … <div className="grid …  bg-surface-soft/35">}`) and the CustomModelsPanel (`{customOpen && <CustomModelsPanel …>}`) mount/unmount with zero transition — a full-width form slab pops open under the row.
findings.proposal: Keep the layout change instant (no height animation) but ease the content into focus: motion.div opacity 0→1 + blur(2px)→0 + y -4→0 at MOTION.row/EASE_OUT on the slab's inner content, fast opacity exit. Same treatment for IntegrationsTab's twin.

findings.current: ServiceRow's token-entry slab (`{editing && !service.connected && <div className="grid grid-cols-[…] bg-surface-soft/35">}`) pops in/out with no transition — identical pattern to ProvidersTab's key editor.
findings.proposal: Apply the same entrance as ProvidersTab once fixed (opacity + blur(2px) + y -4 on content, MOTION.row + EASE_OUT, instant layout); extract the shared "inline key editor" slab if consolidating.

findings.current: ShortcutRecorder swaps its label instantly between "Press chord…" and the formatted accelerator/"Disabled" (L196), and the Reset IconButton (L198-209) pops in/out of the row when the chord differs from default — co-located swaps with no transition.
findings.proposal: Wrap the button label in BlurSwap (swapKey on recording/value) for the in-place text swap; give the Reset button an AnimatePresence opacity+scale 0.96 fade at MOTION.check so the row doesn't jump.

findings.current: Busy-label text swaps are instant across settings: "Checking…"/"Save & reconnect" here, ContextTab.tsx:136 ("Saving…"/"Save changes"/"Saved"), ProvidersTab.tsx:381 ("Working…"), IntegrationsTab.tsx:295/308 ("Connecting…"/"Saving…"), ServerForm.tsx:213 ("Saving…"/"Save"), OAuthStatus.tsx:43 ("…"). Meanwhile CustomModelsPanel already BlurSwaps its Plus↔spinner glyph (ProvidersTab.tsx:600-606) — the established treatment.
findings.proposal: Batch fix: wrap these button labels in BlurSwap keyed on the busy state (default MOTION.check), matching the CustomModelsPanel Add-button precedent, so state changes blur-crossfade instead of flashing text.

findings.current: Primary pressables across settings are raw <button>s with hover-only feedback and no whileTap/:active scale: ProviderRow action (L425-438), Refresh (L269-277), Connect/Cancel (L452-466), Add (L594-608); also ConnectionTab.tsx:46-53, ContextTab.tsx:130-137, IntegrationsTab.tsx:179-187/289-309/497-538, ServerList.tsx:36-43, ServerForm.tsx:114-131/206-214, ServerRow.tsx:71-80, OAuthStatus.tsx:37-44, ArchiveTab.tsx RowAction:128-145, AppearanceTab.tsx ShortcutRecorder:184-197 + PalettePicker trigger:299-308 + VariantCard:396-417. IconButton and .app-row/.sidebar buttons already have active:scale-[0.97] via CSS.
findings.proposal: Batch: add `active:scale-[0.97]` (with `transition-[…,transform] duration-check`) to these buttons, or better, fold the recurring bg-ink CTA / bordered-secondary styles into a shared Button primitive that carries press feedback once — consistent with the IconButton treatment.

findings.current: PrimaryBtn/GhostBtn/DangerBtn (the memory pane's button kit) have hover transitions but no press feedback; same for ClaimBlock's EditorBtn/Save (ClaimBlock.tsx:182-223), GraphView "Center here" (GraphView.tsx:129-137), MemoryGraph "Fit" (MemoryGraph.tsx:449-455), and ClaimsView KindChip (ClaimsView.tsx:196-207).
findings.proposal: Add `active:scale-[0.97]` + `duration-check` to the three shared button components in shared.tsx (covers most call sites in one edit), then the stragglers (EditorBtn, Fit, Center here, KindChip).

vocabulary: This area is the most token-disciplined surface in the app: framer-motion calls consistently pull MOTION.check/palette/panel/route + EASE_EMPHASIZED/EASE_DECELERATE/EASE_OUT and springs (SPRING_MODAL via ENTRY_PANEL for modals, SPRING_LAYOUT for layoutId indicators and toast layout, SPRING_POPOVER for the palette height-morph). Pattern language: instant-by-design palette open/close (duration 0, settled keyboard-frequency taste), directional 16px x-slides with mode="wait" for hierarchy/tab page swaps (PaletteBody mirrors TabPanels verbatim), FLIP layoutId pills for tab indicators, and press = scale 0.97 at duration-check. The CSS side uses var(--duration-*) religiously but pairs them with plain `ease`/`ease-out` keywords (and Tailwind's default `ease-out`) instead of the --ease-emphasized/--ease-out-soft vars — a systemic keyword-vs-token ease drift; bare Tailwind `transition-colors` (default 150ms, cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1)) also sneaks in off the 120/140ms scale. Notable v4 trap discovered here: `active:scale-[0.97]` sets the standalone `scale` property, which `transition-[...,transform]` does not cover.

findings.current: Pill indicator animates `width` via CSS: `width: pill ? pill.w + pillGrow * 2 : 0` with `transition: ready ? \`transform var(--duration-panel) ${EASE}, width var(--duration-panel) ${EASE}\` : "none"` — width is a layout prop, re-laid-out and repainted every frame on each segment switch. The component also hand-rolls offsetLeft/offsetWidth measurement + ResizeObserver + rAF ready-gating to drive it.
findings.proposal: Replace the measured pill with a motion layoutId indicator exactly like Tabs.tsx's pill variant (motion.span layoutId + SPRING_LAYOUT) — FLIP animates position AND size as compositor transforms with radius correction, and deletes the measurement/observer plumbing. Also consolidates two parallel pill-indicator implementations into one.

findings.current: `transition-[background-color,border-color,box-shadow,color,transform] duration-check ease-out ... active:scale-[0.97]` — in Tailwind v4, `scale-[0.97]` sets the standalone `scale` property, which is NOT covered by `transform` in the transition-property list. Press feedback snaps in/out with zero easing instead of the tokenized duration-check ease the class intends (.app-row CSS already transitions `scale` explicitly for this exact reason).
findings.proposal: Add `scale` to the arbitrary transition list: `transition-[background-color,border-color,box-shadow,color,transform,scale]` (or swap `transform`→`scale` since nothing animates transform here). One-word fix restores eased press/release on a core primitive.

findings.current: Same v4 scale/transform mismatch as Chip: `transition-[background-color,color,transform] duration-check ease-out ... active:scale-[0.97]` — the `scale` property isn't in the transition list, so every icon-button press snaps untransitioned.
findings.proposal: Include `scale` in the transition-property list: `transition-[background-color,color,scale]` (transform itself is never animated here).

findings.current: `transition: transform var(--duration-panel) cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1), ...` — raw back-out overshoot bezier inlined in CSS; not registered alongside --ease-emphasized / --ease-out-soft, and no EASE_* mirror exists in motion.ts.
findings.proposal: Register it as a named var (e.g. `--ease-back-out: cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1)`) next to the other --ease-* tokens with a matching EASE_BACK_OUT mirror in motion.ts, and reference the var here. Keeps the deliberate macOS-style knob overshoot but makes the curve reusable and visible in the token inventory.

findings.current: `transition-colors duration-300 ease-out` — 300ms is Tailwind's default scale, off the token scale (check 120 / row 140 / palette 180 / trace 200 / panel 220 / route 360). The comment documents it as a deliberately gentle crossfade for the 1.5px dot.
findings.proposal: Promote the deliberate value into the token scale (e.g. `--duration-gentle: 300ms` + MOTION mirror) and use `duration-gentle`, or settle on `duration-panel` (220ms) if the gap to panel isn't perceptible at this size. Either way the value stops living outside the scale.

findings.current: `const BACKDROP_DURATION = 0; const PANEL_DURATION = 0; const EASE = EASE_OUT;` then `transition={{ duration: PANEL_DURATION, ease: EASE, layout: SPRING_POPOVER }}` — local zero-duration literals driving no-op opacity tweens. Instant open/close is correct settled taste (keyboard-frequency surface, do NOT animate it), but the dead fade scaffolding plus an ease that can never apply reads as drift.
findings.proposal: Keep instant open/close; strip the no-op tween: drop BACKDROP_DURATION/PANEL_DURATION/EASE and the animate/exit opacity props, keep AnimatePresence purely for unmount and `transition={{ layout: SPRING_POPOVER }}` for the height morph, with a one-line comment that open/close is intentionally instant.

findings.current: `PAGE_VARIANTS` is a verbatim copy of TabPanels' `VARIANTS` (enter x: dir*16 / exit x: dir*-16) — the comment even says it mirrors TabPanels. The directional mode="wait" slide itself is settled taste; only the duplication is the issue: the 16px offset and curve now live in two literals that can drift independently.
findings.proposal: Export the directional slide variants once (from ui/TabPanels.tsx or tokens/motion.ts, e.g. `SLIDE_PAGE_VARIANTS`) and import in PaletteBody so palette pages and tab panels stay on one literal.

findings.current: `transition={{ duration: MOTION.check, ease: EASE_EMPHASIZED, delay: i * 0.02 }}` — the shared transition prop applies to exit too, so popping the crumb at depth i waits i*20ms before its exit even starts (deepest crumbs are the ones being popped). Stagger literal `0.02` is also untokenized.
findings.proposal: Scope the stagger to entrances only: move `delay` into an enter-scoped transition (e.g. variants with per-variant transition, or `exit` carrying `transition: { duration: MOTION.check, ease: EASE_EMPHASIZED }` without delay). Exits should lead, not lag.

findings.current: Bare Tailwind `transition-colors` with no duration/ease utilities in three palette spots: Row.tsx:33 (icon tile tint), Breadcrumbs.tsx:39 (crumb chip hover), CommandPicker.tsx:135 (icon tint). No `--default-transition-duration` override exists, so these ride Tailwind's defaults — 150ms + cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1) — off the token scale (check 120 / row 140) and off the --ease-* curves, while sibling primitives (Chip, IconButton, .app-row) explicitly use duration-check ease-out.
findings.proposal: Append `duration-check ease-out` (the established Chip/IconButton convention) to each, or set Tailwind v4 theme defaults (`--default-transition-duration: var(--duration-check)`) once in styles.css so bare utilities inherit the scale.

findings.current: ToastCard is a clickable `motion.button` (navigates to session / opens automations) with layout + enter/exit animation but no whileTap and no active:scale — presses give zero feedback.
findings.proposal: Add `whileTap={{ scale: 0.97 }}` with SPRING_TAP (matches the Chip/IconButton 0.97 convention).

findings.current: DefaultFallback hand-rolls its two buttons: "Try again" uses `hover:opacity-90 transition-opacity` (opacity hover is off-vocabulary — everything else shifts color/bg) and "Reload window" uses bare `transition-colors`; both ride Tailwind's default 150ms and neither has press feedback.
findings.proposal: Batch fix: add `active:scale-[0.97] duration-check ease-out` (plus `scale` in the transition list) to both, and swap the primary's opacity-hover for a bg shift — or reuse the shared button styling instead of bespoke classes. Low priority (rare surface), but it is the screen where polish signals care.

findings.current: EmptyState renders fully static. It occupies the same slot as message segments in Messages.tsx — TurnGroup rows enter with the SPRING_ROW_ENTRY stagger, so switching to an empty/new session hard-cuts the hero icon + heading in while switching to a populated session animates content in. Inconsistent siblings in the same slot.
findings.proposal: Give it the standard section entrance once on mount: opacity 0→1, y 8→0, blur(3px)→0 with MOTION.panel + EASE_DECELERATE (initial mount only, no exit — it's replaced by content). Skip if session switching is judged keyboard-frequency for this user; then the right move is instead documenting it as intentionally instant.

vocabulary: The CSS/token layer runs a seven-stop duration scale (100/120/140/180/200/220/360ms) that is perfectly mirrored by MOTION in motion.ts — zero numeric drift — with a clear tiering in practice: check(120) for press/hover color shifts, row(140)/palette(180) for chrome, panel(220) for state changes, route(360) reserved for pane slides. The ease vocabulary is much thinner than the JS side: only --ease-emphasized and --ease-out-soft exist as CSS vars (motion.ts has five eases), so styles.css falls back to keyword ease/ease-out/ease-in-out plus a few raw cubic-beziers, and components overwhelmingly use Tailwind's builtin ease-out (24x) for hover-tier transitions. The dominant component pattern is disciplined: explicit transition-[prop,prop] lists + duration tokens (transition-all has ZERO occurrences app-wide), press feedback via active:scale 0.96-0.985, and ambient "still working" loops (shine sweep, breathe halo, comet rim) at untokenized 1.2-3.6s cadences. Reduced motion is handled on both sides (MotionConfig reducedMotion="user" in App.tsx plus a universal CSS animation/transition stripper).

findings.current: transition: box-shadow var(--duration-row) cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); — raw bezier inlined in .surface-panel and again in .surface-card. The value is EASE_HOVER from motion.ts, but no --ease-hover CSS var exists; motion.ts's header says the CSS side 'must stay in sync', yet EASE_STANDARD, EASE_DECELERATE, and EASE_HOVER have no CSS counterparts at all.
findings.proposal: Register --ease-hover: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) in the @theme block (alongside --ease-emphasized/--ease-out-soft) and reference var(--ease-hover) in both rules. Optionally add --ease-standard/--ease-decelerate too so the CSS layer mirrors all five motion.ts eases and drift is impossible.

findings.current: .sidebar button transition: scale var(--duration-check) cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1) — this bezier is byte-for-byte the CSS 'ease' keyword, written out as a literal.
findings.proposal: Replace with the ease keyword, or better, var(--ease-out-soft) to match the press-feedback ease used by .app-row and the rest of the press vocabulary.

findings.current: .switch-control-knob transition: transform var(--duration-panel) cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) — an untokenized back-out overshoot ease, the only overshoot curve in the system (deliberate macOS-switch feel per the comment).
findings.proposal: Tokenize as --ease-back-out in @theme (with an EASE_BACK_OUT mirror in motion.ts if any JS consumer appears). The overshoot itself is fine taste for a switch knob; the literal is the issue.

findings.current: --animate-roll-in: rollIn 160ms cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1); --animate-fade-in: fadeIn 200ms ease-out; --animate-pop-in: popIn 180ms cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1); --animate-pulse-soft: pulseSoft 1.2s ease-in-out infinite; — hardcoded ms plus the --ease-out-soft bezier inlined twice. 160ms is off the duration scale entirely (between row 140 and palette 180); 200/180 duplicate --duration-trace/--duration-palette as literals.
findings.proposal: Reference the vars: rollIn var(--duration-row or --duration-palette) var(--ease-out-soft), fadeIn var(--duration-trace) var(--ease-out-soft), popIn var(--duration-palette) var(--ease-out-soft). Snap rollIn's 160ms to a scale stop — it is the only off-scale UI duration in the CSS layer.

findings.current: Ambient loop cadences are all raw literals: pulseSoft 1.2s, statusBreathe 2s, skeleton-shimmer 1.4s, streamingCodeSheen 2.4s, shineText 2.2s, thinking-rim 1.8s/2.6s/3.6s (at least locally tokenized via --thinking-duration). The duration scale tops out at 360ms — there is no ambient tier, so every loop invents its own cadence.
findings.proposal: Either add an ambient tier to the token scale (e.g. --duration-breathe: 2s, --duration-shimmer: 1.4s) and sweep the loops onto it, or add an explicit note in motion.ts/styles.css that infinite-loop cadences are exempt from the scale — right now they read as drift against the TOKENS ONLY rule.

findings.current: Main chat pane: transition-[left,right] duration-route ease-emphasized with left-[var(--sidebar-width,272px)] / data-driven left-0 / right-[320px] (line 126), plus transition-[padding-left] duration-route on the chat header (line 53). Sidebar toggles animate the absolute left/right offsets and padding of the entire message column — full-subtree relayout every frame for 360ms.
findings.proposal: Per the app's own GPU-props rule (and the styles.css line 384 comment claiming 'panels slide behind them via x transform'): slide the sidebars with transform and let the chat pane reflow once at the end (or snapshot-and-translate). At minimum, profile frame timing during sidebar toggle with a long message list — this is the largest layout-prop animation in the app and the route slide will drop frames on heavy sessions.

findings.current: All composer thinking-rim variants animate paint-tier properties in infinite loops while the agent runs: comet rotates a Houdini --snake-angle conic-gradient (repaint per frame; the comment at 1198-1200 contains an explicit TODO: 'the real perf win for this animation is gating it via useInView when modals occlude the composer'), breath loops box-shadow on the whole composer card (1249-1258), hue-cycle loops border-color (1268-1274).
findings.proposal: Implement the code's own TODO: gate data-thinking when the composer is occluded (modal open / window blurred). For breath, pre-render the halo on a ::after (static box-shadow or blurred radial gradient) and loop only its opacity/transform — same visual, compositor-only.

findings.current: .streaming-code sheen: background-size 240% gradient with animation: streamingCodeSheen 2.4s ease-in-out infinite animating background-position across the entire pre block — full-block repaint per frame, running concurrently with streaming text relayout. Long code blocks make the repaint area large exactly when the main thread is busiest.
findings.proposal: Move the sheen to an absolutely-positioned overlay (::before with overflow:hidden on .code-block) animated via transform: translateX — the exact GPU-composited pattern .skeleton::after (lines 153-176) already uses in this same file.

findings.current: @keyframes statusBreathe animates box-shadow (two-layer currentColor halo) 2s ease-in-out infinite on .status-dot-breathe — paint-tier loop, acknowledged in the comment ('halo expands and contracts via box-shadow alone'). Paint area is tiny (6px dot + halo) but it runs indefinitely on every live-status dot, and lists can show several at once.
findings.proposal: Render the halo as a pre-blurred ::after (radial-gradient, currentColor) and loop opacity + scale instead — compositor-only, identical look. Low urgency given the tiny paint area.

findings.current: transition-colors duration-300 ease-out — the single Tailwind literal duration class in the entire src tree. The adjacent comment defends 300ms as 'deliberately gentle... slower than the interactive MOTION scale on purpose' for the 1.5px dot.
findings.proposal: The reasoning is sound but the value should live on the scale: add a gentle/ambient stop (e.g. --duration-gentle: 300ms) to @theme + MOTION and use duration-gentle, so the one intentional off-scale duration is visible in the token file instead of buried in a component.

findings.current: Tailwind builtin ease-out class (cubic-bezier(0, 0, 0.2, 1) — not a token value) used for hover/press color transitions: ApprovalBanner.tsx x7, composer/BudgetDial.tsx x5, agents/AgentRunRow.tsx x2, and x1 each in sidebar/RowAction.tsx, automations/AutomationEditor.tsx, StatusDot.tsx, QuickCapture.tsx, Message.tsx, Mermaid.tsx, IconButton.tsx, ComposerSelectors.tsx, Composer.tsx, Chip.tsx. Meanwhile the registered token utilities ease-emphasized / ease-out-soft are generated by @theme and already used in Chat.tsx. This is a third, undocumented ease in the de facto vocabulary.
findings.proposal: Batch decision: either bless builtin ease-out for the check-tier hover/press pattern by documenting it in motion.ts (cheapest — the feel is fine at 120ms), or sweep all 24 to ease-out-soft for a single ease vocabulary. Don't leave it implicit.

findings.current: Dead/bypassed animation tokens: --animate-pop-in + @keyframes popIn have zero consumers anywhere; --animate-pulse-soft + @keyframes pulseSoft also have zero consumers, while BudgetDial.tsx:162 uses Tailwind's builtin animate-pulse (2s opacity-only) instead of the registered 1.2s scale+opacity pulse-soft token for the over-budget warning.
findings.proposal: Delete --animate-pop-in/popIn (dead code per project rules). For pulse-soft: either switch BudgetDial to animate-pulse-soft (the token was presumably tuned for exactly this) or delete the token and keep the builtin — one pulse, not two.

findings.current: composerJustSent send acknowledgement: [data-just-sent="true"]::after paints a top-down ink-gradient fill that flashes and fades over var(--duration-route) (360ms) on every message submit. GPU-safe (opacity-only on a pseudo) and tokenized, but it is a one-shot surface-flash ack on a keyboard-frequency interaction, and it borrows the route token (reserved for pane slides) for a decorative pulse — both at odds with rule 7 (flash acks, >300ms UI durations) and the app's documented restraint taste.
findings.proposal: Flag for taste review: if the send-ack flash is settled, shorten it to panel-tier (220ms) so the slowest duration in the system stays reserved for routes; otherwise remove it — the composer clearing + thinking rim starting already acknowledge the send.

findings.current: The universal reduced-motion stripper (animation-duration: 0.001ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important on *) freezes the 13 animate-spin loading spinners at frame 0 — under prefers-reduced-motion a static spinner icon is the only loading cue, ambiguous for indeterminate waits. Otherwise reduced-motion coverage is solid app-wide (MotionConfig reducedMotion="user" in App.tsx:172 + this stripper + targeted blocks at 177-184 and 488-492, which are now redundant with the stripper).
findings.proposal: Exempt essential loading indicators from the stripper (e.g. .animate-spin or a [data-essential-motion] escape hatch keeps its animation — rotation of a small glyph is conventionally allowed under reduced motion since it conveys state, not decoration). While touching the block, drop the now-redundant targeted resets at 177-184/488-492.
